SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.fx.controls

Interface ColumnDef<S,T>

Type Parameters:
S - The type of the objects displayed in the rows of the table or grid.
T - The type of the values contained within the cells of this column.
All Known Implementing Classes:
ColumnDefGeneric, ColumnDefText
public sealed interface ColumnDef<S,T> permits ColumnDefText<S,T>, ColumnDefGeneric<S,T>
Represents the definition of a column in a tabular data structure. This interface is intended to be implemented by specific column definitions, defining behaviors for retrieving and setting cell values, as well as additional properties such as the column's header and editability status.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final double
    DEFAULT_WEIGHT
    A constant defining the default weight of a column.

    Creates a new instance of ColumnDefGenericBuilder for defining the properties of a column with custom cell rendering logic.

    double

    Retrieves the weight of the column, which represents its relative proportion or allocation of space compared to other columns.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_WEIGHT

      static final double DEFAULT_WEIGHT
      A constant defining the default weight of a column. The weight determines the relative allocation of space for the column in comparison to other columns within a table.

      The default value of 1.0 indicates an equal proportional distribution among columns with the same weight.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • builder

      static <S, T> ColumnDefTextBuilder<S,T> builder(String name, javafx.util.StringConverter<T> converter)
      Creates a new instance of ColumnDefTextBuilder.
      Type Parameters:
      S - The type of the objects displayed in the rows of the table or grid.
      T - The type of the values contained within the cells of this column.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the column, representing its header text.
      converter - A StringConverter responsible for converting between the cell value type and its string representation.
      Returns:
      A new ColumnDefTextBuilder instance for configuring and building a text-based column definition.

    • builder

      static <S, T> ColumnDefGenericBuilder<S,T> builder(String name, BiFunction<@Nullable javafx.scene.Node,T,@Nullable javafx.scene.Node> nodeFactory)
      Creates a new instance of ColumnDefGenericBuilder for defining the properties of a column with custom cell rendering logic.
      Type Parameters:
      S - The type of the objects displayed in the rows of the table or grid.
      T - The type of the values contained within the cells of this column.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the column, which is typically used as the header text.
      nodeFactory - A BiFunction that takes an optional Node (representing an existing cell node or null) and a value of type T, then produces an optional Node to display the value.
      Returns:
      A ColumnDefGenericBuilder instance for further configuration of the column.

    • get

      T get(S row)
      Retrieves the value of the cell in this column for the specified row object.
      Parameters:
      row - The row object from which the value should be extracted.
      Returns:
      The value contained within the cell corresponding to this column for the given row.

    • set

      void set(S row, T value)
      Sets the value for a specific cell in the column, corresponding to the given row.
      Parameters:
      row - The row object representing the data entry whose cell value is being updated.
      value - The new value to be set in the cell of the column for the specified row.

    • text

      String text()
      Retrieves the header text of the column.
      Returns:
      A string representing the header text of this column.

    • graphic

      Optional<javafx.scene.Node> graphic()
      Retrieves the header text of the column.
      Returns:
      A string representing the header text of this column.

    • editable

      boolean editable()
      Determines whether the cells in this column are editable.
      Returns:
      true if the cells in the column can be edited; false otherwise.

    • minWidth

      double minWidth()
      Retrieves the minimum width of the column.
      Returns:
      The minimum width of the column as a double value.

    • maxWidth

      double maxWidth()
      Retrieves the maximum width of the column. This value defines the upper limit for the column's width when rendering or resizing within a table or grid.
      Returns:
      The maximum width of the column as a double.

    • weight

      double weight()
      Retrieves the weight of the column, which represents its relative proportion or allocation of space compared to other columns.
      Returns:
      The weight of this column as a double value.

    • setWeight

      void setWeight(double weight)
      Set a new weight value.
      Parameters:
      weight - The new weight value.

    • resizable

      boolean resizable()
      Indicates whether the column can be resized.
      Returns:
      true if the column is resizable; false otherwise.

    • reorderable

      boolean reorderable()
      Indicates whether the column can be reordered (dragged to another position using the mouse).
      Returns:
      true if the column is reorderable; false otherwise.

    • sortable

      boolean sortable()
      Indicates whether the column is sortable.
      Returns:
      true if the column is sortable; false otherwise.

    • valueGetter

      Function<S,T> valueGetter()
      Retrieves the function used to extract the cell value from the row object.
      Returns:
      A function that takes a row object of type S and returns the cell value of type T.

    • valueSetter

      BiConsumer<S,T> valueSetter()
      Retrieves the value setter function for this column. The value setter is a BiConsumer that updates the cell value of a row object.
      Returns:
      a BiConsumer that sets the cell value in a row object, accepting the row object and the new value as parameters.
